text: I find myself drawn into a space devoted to research and development—a world where ideas and prototypes blend. The bold letters R+D evoke both curiosity and purpose. As I read the poetic reflection on the display, I feel both detached and fascinated, as though I am both observer and participant, drifting between understanding technology and pondering its impact. The artificial lighting and modern design make the air feel clinical, yet the undercurrent of human creativity brings warmth. My mind oscillates between awe at what is possible and wariness about what it means to be human amid machines.
